4 Quality Information Document for OMI_CLIMATE_TEMPSAL_BALTIC_Stz_trend 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-BAL-OMI-QUID-CLIMATE-
TEMPSAL-Stz-trend  
Issue 4.0 – November 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
OMI_CLIMATE_TEMPSAL_BALTIC_Stz_trend. 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
This document provides to Copernicus Marine Service users information on the quality 
of the horizontally averaged salinity trend for the Baltic Sea. The linear trends/slopes/fits 
and their 95% confidence intervals have been calculated usi ng last square 
regression method described in Draper and Smith (1998) eq. 1.2.9 and 1.4.8.  
The daily salinity data is extracted from the BALTICSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_003_011 
product. The accuracy of the index is directly dependent on the accuracy of the Baltic 
reanalysis product (BALTICSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_003_011) therefore evaluation is based 
on the QUID documents of the aforementioned product. 
c) Copernicus Marine products used as input for the computation 
Product name (and Type) Documentation 
BALTICSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_003
_011 (model reanalysis) 
QUID: 
https://catalogue.marine.copernicus.eu/documents/
QUID/CMEMS-BAL-QUID-003-011.pdf 
PUM: 
https://catalogue.marine.copernicus.eu/documents/P
UM/CMEMS-BAL-PUM-003-011_012.pdf 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
5 Quality Information Document for OMI_CLIMATE_TEMPSAL_BALTIC_Stz_trend 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-BAL-OMI-QUID-CLIMATE-
TEMPSAL-Stz-trend  
Issue 4.0 – November 2025 
2) SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION  
a) Subsurface salinity trend  
In the CMEMS-BAL-QUID-003-011 the salinity of reanalysis product has been compared 
with all available measurements across the Baltic for period of 1993-2024.  As the data 
assimilation is employed the model results of reanalysis show only small biases (<0. 3 
g/kg) compare to observations at all observed depth ranges. The RMSE showed values 
around 0.48 – 0.73 g/kg in the layer of 0-80 m and decreased toward the deeper layers 
to up to 0.28 g/kg in the layer of 80-220 m.  
The linear trends/slopes/fits and their 95% confidence intervals (Figure 1) have been 
calculated using last square regression  method described in Draper and Smith (1998) 
eq. 1.2.9 and 1.4.8.  
Scientific evaluation can be fo und in the Copernicus Marine Service Ocean S

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document evaluates the quality of horizontally averaged salinity trend for the Baltic Sea, providing details on the methodology and data sources used in its computation.

product_line.name: Baltic Sea Climate Salinity Trend Evaluation Report
